validation.answerGuidance: Describe: (1) what specific data condition triggers the failure, (2) who detects it, (3) what the actor does in response, (4) how the process resumes or terminates.

keywords: searches the database for the item
search_lookup.answerGuidance: Describe: (1) what the actor searches for and what goes wrong, (2) whether they can proceed with partial information, (3) how the process continues.

data_input.answerGuidance: Describe: (1) what specific data problem occurs, (2) whether the system or actor detects it, (3) what correction or fallback is available.

keywords: supports the assignment of an adjuster
resource_assignment.answerGuidance: Describe: (1) why the resource is unavailable, (2) whether a default or fallback is assigned automatically, (3) what happens if no assignment occurs within a time limit.

system_interaction.answerGuidance: Describe: (1) the nature of the failure, (2) whether work in progress is preserved, (3) what the actor does while waiting or after the failure.

completion.answerGuidance: Describe: (1) what minimum information is required to finish, (2) what the system does if requirements are not met, (3) whether the actor can save and return later.

keywords: comes back to finish the entry
save_resume.answerGuidance: Describe: (1) whether progress can be saved, (2) what data is preserved, (3) how resume works and whether re-validation is required.

vague_condition.answerGuidance: Describe: (1) the exact state, actor action, or system signal that causes execution to leave the normal flow and enter this branch, (2) which step in the normal flow this branch forks from and why, (3) what the actor or system does as the first step of the branch, (4) whether the branch re-joins the normal flow or terminates.

user_cancellation.answerGuidance: Describe: (1) at which step the actor can cancel, (2) what data or state is discarded, (3) whether the actor is prompted for confirmation, (4) how the system notifies relevant parties.

alternative_path.answerGuidance: Describe: (1) what triggers the actor to take this path instead of the main flow, (2) how the steps differ, (3) whether this path re-joins the main flow or terminates independently.

authorization_denial.answerGuidance: Describe: (1) who denies the authorization and under what condition, (2) what feedback the actor receives, (3) whether a re-request or appeal path is available.

keywords: no response is received within the deadline
the system does not respond in time
timeout_retry.answerGuidance: Describe: (1) what operation times out and after how long, (2) how many retries are attempted, (3) what the actor or system does after exhausting retries.

notification_failure.answerGuidance: Describe: (1) what notification fails to deliver, (2) whether the system retries delivery, (3) whether the recipient is alerted through an alternative channel, (4) whether the failure is logged or escalated.

eligibility_failure.answerGuidance: Describe: (1) the exact validation failure trigger, (2) whether the claim is declined immediately, (3) what claimant notifications and records are required, (4) whether the process terminates or allows retry/appeal.

assignment_unavailability.answerGuidance: Describe: (1) what happens when no assignee is available, (2) whether the case is queued/on hold, (3) who is notified, (4) how and when assignment resumes.

policy_outcome.answerGuidance: Describe: (1) how severe vs minor policy violations are distinguished, (2) which path declines/terminates, (3) which path allows negotiation or adjusted payout, (4) who decides each outcome.
